A test script for nested try-catch blocks in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a short script that demonstrates the use of nested `try-catch` blocks in JavaScript. The inner `try` block throws a string "error", which is caught by the inner `catch` block and re-thrown. The outer `try-catch` block catches the re-thrown error, preventing it from propagating further. The `assert(false);` statement is unreachable due to the re-throwing of the error, illustrating control flow interruption. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on error handling and control flow in JavaScript.
